what_it_does: This noticeboard item argues that the company repository is already a dual code-and-Obsidian-vault asset and summarizes the existing vault conventions that a board-as-vault idea can build on.
observations: Cites /home/tim/company/docs/vault-conventions.md as the canonical Vault Conventions spec, tagged obsidian.
States the repo is simultaneously a code repository and an Obsidian vault where documentation is the knowledge graph.
Every folder is expected to have an AGENTS.md constitution-note with YAML frontmatter.
Wikilinks carry relations between constitutions via unique aliases, since files are all named AGENTS.md.
MAP.md (alias Company Map) is the vault home with a rendered relationship graph and an auto-maintained REGISTRY block.
A two-vault boundary is declared: ~/company is built reality and a build-prep/ counterpart vault is design intent.
Cross-vault references are by prose name because Obsidian does not resolve links across vaults.
Freshness is owned by a Coherence Gate commit-time check.
AGENTS.md line 58 and MAP.md line 15 are said to state the repo is also an Obsidian vault.
The board-as-vault idea is framed as inheriting this mostly-built convention layer.
standouts: The repo is treated as both a code repository and an Obsidian vault.
Per-folder AGENTS.md constitutions with YAML frontmatter and alias-based wikilinks.
MAP.md as a home note with graph and auto-maintained registry.
A Coherence Gate freshness check at commit time.
conventions: Markdown noticeboard item with YAML frontmatter and id/address metadata.
Vault Conventions document as canonical spec for repo/vault duality.
Wikilink relation grammar using unique aliases.
MAP.md home note with rendered graph and REGISTRY block.
Two-vault boundary between built reality and design intent.
concerns: The title appears truncated at on pap.
Line-number claims about AGENTS.md and MAP.md cannot be verified from this file alone.
The referenced external file content is not included, so the claims are unverified here.
No evidence in this file shows that the Coherence Gate is actually implemented or enforced.
notes: A brief claiming the repo already has a full Obsidian-vault convention and that the board-as-vault concept should plug into it.
questions: What is the exact YAML schema for AGENTS.md frontmatter?
What does the Coherence Gate actually check at commit time?
Where is the build-prep/ counterpart vault located?
Are the cited line numbers in AGENTS.md and MAP.md still current?
What is the precise scope of the board-as-vault idea being supported?
What does P3 refer to in the title?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the title and body both aim to show that the repository already contains a vault/knowledge-graph convention the host idea can reuse.
intention_signals: It is trying to lower the perceived effort of a board-as-vault proposal by showing that the underlying conventions, link grammar, home note, and freshness gate already exist in the repository.
novelty: The explicit dual identity of a code repository as an Obsidian vault, with per-folder AGENTS.md constitutions, wikilink-driven relations, a MAP.md registry, and a two-vault reality/intent boundary.
contribution: It records that an existing repo-as-vault convention is already in force, including per-folder AGENTS.md constitutions, wikilink relation grammar, a MAP.md home note with registry and graph, a two-vault boundary, and a Coherence Gate freshness concept, which a fused system could preserve as prior convention.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item documenting that the company repository already operates as a code repository and Obsidian vault with AGENTS.md constitutions, MAP.md registry, wikilink relations, and a Coherence Gate freshness check.
intention_for_embedding: To establish that a proposed board-as-vault feature can inherit an already-designed vault convention rather than inventing one.
